options.a: It is a flow of warm air up a slope of a hill caused by surface heating.
options.b: It is an adiabatically heated wind blowing down a mountain side. The temperature on the lee side is normally higher than on the windward side of the mountain at the same level.
options.c: It is an adiabatically heated wind blowing down a mountain side. The temperature on the lee side is normally lower than on the windward side of the mountain at the same level.
options.d: It is a warm and moist anabatic wind that usually carries precipitation.
